Class Audio

java.lang.Object
org.obsidium.audio.Audio
public class Audio extends Object
A class for playing .wav audio files.

To use the functionality of this audio class, it needs to be instantiated.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • Audio(String path)

  • Constructor Details

    • Audio

      public Audio(String path)
      The constructor of the audio class.
      Parameters:
      path - of the .wav audio file that you want to load in
      Since:
      1.0

  • Method Details

    • isLoaded

      public boolean isLoaded()
      Returns:
      true if the audio file is loaded in.
      Since:
      1.0

    • isPlaying

      public boolean isPlaying()
      Returns:
      true if the audio file is playing.
      Since:
      1.0

    • play

      public void play(int loop)
      Plays and loops the audio.

      If the audio isn't loaded in, it will do nothing. Otherwise it will loop how many times you have specified it to. If the argument is -1, it will loop indefinitely.

      Parameters:
      loop - loops the audio a specified set of times, if -1 it will loop indefinitely
      Since:
      1.0

    • play

      public void play()
      Plays the audio.

      If the audio isn't loaded in, it will do nothing. Otherwise it will begin playing 1 time.

      Since:
      1.0

    • toggle

      public void toggle()
      Toggles between pausing/resuming.
      Since:
      1.0

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the music that is currently playing.

      If resume() is called after the audio has stopped, it will just continue playing.

      If the audio has not been loaded in, it will do nothing. You can check if the audio has been loaded in with this isLoaded() method.

      Since:
      1.0

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Pauses the audio that is currently playing.

      If the song has not been loaded in, it will do nothing. You can check if the audio has been loaded in with this isLoaded() method.

      Since:
      1.0

    • resume

      public void resume()
      Resumes the audio that is currently playing.

      If the audio has not been loaded in, it will do nothing. You can check if the audio has been loaded in with this isLoaded() method.

    • setVolume

      public void setVolume(float volume)
      Sets the volume of the audio.

      If the audio has not been loaded in, it will do nothing. You can check if the audio has been loaded in with this isLoaded() method.

      Parameters:
      volume - a value between 0 - 1
      Since:
      1.0

    • getVolume

      public float getVolume()
      Returns the volume of the audio.

      The audio will be between 0 and 1.

      Returns:
      volume
      Since:
      1.0

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes and releases the memory of the audio.
      Since:
      1.0
